{{Infobox location
| name = House
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[House Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The House, also known as [[Valinhall]], is an incomprehensibly vast and self-contained entity that functions as its own world. Unlike the standard dwellings found in villages like [[Myria]], which are constructed from wood or clay bricks, [[the House]] contains entire worlds within its walls and lacks external doors or windows. It is a dynamic environment that can rearrange its layout, adding new rooms to meet the requirements of those living within it. The interior is incredibly diverse, ranging from grand, expensive chambers and vast throne rooms to rough, cave-like spaces, endless icy fields, and even entire cities like [[Elysia]]. Some areas of [[the House]] are designed for specific purposes, such as gardens that provide essential food and water or specialized council rooms, while other massive spaces serve no apparent function. The House is also home to various inhabitants and can be dangerous, with some suggesting it may even attempt to kill those inside. Certain individuals, such as [[the Nye]], are fundamentally bound to it. Historically, powerful figures have ruled [[the House]], providing its inhabitants with names and structure. The entity is capable of significant physical change, including the ability to collapse entirely.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The House is a unique structure that possesses no outside. Within its boundaries, an artificial sky hangs over a massive fruit tree and a pool capable of healing wounds. [[The Nye]] are bound to this location, which has provided [[Simon]] with various prizes. Ultimately, [[Simon]] contemplates the possibility of escaping into [[the House]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[House]] has no outside.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[House]] contains a pool that can heal wounds.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[House]] features an artificial sky and a huge fruit tree.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] has prizes from [[the House]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12">''House of Blades'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Nye]] are bound to [[the House]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c18">''House of Blades'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] considers escaping into [[the House]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The House is an incomprehensibly vast realm whose entry hall can be seen through the Gate. Within its boundaries, [[Simon]] once ran through various halls, and it serves as a location associated with [[Kai]] and others. While the [[Incarnation]] possesses an unlimited supply of nearly all [[the House]]'s powers, certain members of [[the House]] view the actions of [[Valinhall]] as being aligned with the [[Incarnation]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[House]]'s entry hall is visible through the Gate.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Some members of [[the House]] would see [[Valinhall]]'s actions as being on the [[Incarnation]]'s side.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] has access to almost all of [[the House]]'s powers in unlimited supply.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[House]] is described as incomprehensibly vast.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[House]] is a place that [[Kai]] and others are associated with.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[House]] contains halls that [[Simon]] ran through.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The House served as a training ground for [[Simon]] and was once a place where [[Kai]] spent his days lounging around. During his travels, [[Simon]] passes through the location, though it is noted that the power he calls upon does not originate from [[the House]] itself. Within its walls, [[Kai]] possesses the ability to banish [[Mithra]], and [[Leah]] identifies [[the House]] as a realm she can use to follow the [[King]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] can banish [[Mithra]] within [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House]] is a place where [[Simon]] received training.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House]] is a location [[Simon]] passes through.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] mentions [[the House]] as a place she can follow the [[King]] into.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] used to lounge around [[the House]] all day.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The power [[Simon]] is calling from is not [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“He can follow me into the House,” she said.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
